title: Write a commit-msg Hook
description: Write a commit-msg hook script that enforces conventional commit format, and configure a useful Git alias.
starterCode:
#!/bin/sh
# (1) Write a commit-msg hook that rejects commits not matching Conventional Commits format
# The hook receives the commit message file path as $1
# Pattern to match: type(scope): description
# Types: feat, fix, docs, chore, refactor, test, style
# Exit 1 to reject; exit 0 to allow

# YOUR HOOK SCRIPT HERE

# (2) Create a global git alias for a pretty graph log
# YOUR GIT CONFIG COMMAND HERE

# (3) Set VS Code as the global git editor
# YOUR GIT CONFIG COMMAND HERE
solutionCode:
#!/bin/sh
# .git/hooks/commit-msg
# Enforce conventional commit format
COMMIT_MSG=$(cat "$1")
PATTERN="^(feat|fix|docs|chore|refactor|test|style)(\(.+\))?: .+"
if ! echo "$COMMIT_MSG" | grep -qE "$PATTERN"; then
  echo "ERROR: Commit message does not follow Conventional Commits format."
  echo "Expected: type(scope): description"
  echo "Example:  feat(auth): add OAuth2 login"
  exit 1
fi
exit 0

# Set global alias for pretty log
git config --global alias.lg "log --oneline --graph --decorate --all"

# Set VS Code as git editor
git config --global core.editor "code --wait"
hints: Start the hook script with #!/bin/sh as the shebang line
Read the commit message using: COMMIT_MSG=$(cat "$1") — the hook receives the file path as $1
Use grep -qE with your pattern to test the message silently; combine with ! to detect a mismatch
A non-zero exit (exit 1) aborts the commit with your error message; exit 0 allows it
Use git config --global alias.<name> "<command>" to create aliases

validationRules.errorMessage: Start the hook script with a shebang (#!/bin/sh or #!/bin/bash)
Include conventional commit type keywords in the validation pattern
Exit with code 1 to reject invalid commits

simulatedOutput.success:
Hook script validated successfully!

Testing hook with valid message 'feat(auth): add login'... PASSED ✓
Testing hook with invalid message 'added stuff'... REJECTED ✓ (exit 1)

Alias 'lg' created: git lg now shows pretty graph log
Editor set to VS Code

Hook is ready at .git/hooks/commit-msg (remember: chmod +x .git/hooks/commit-msg)
simulatedOutput.failure: ERROR: Validation failed. Make sure your script has a shebang (#!/bin/sh), includes conventional commit type keywords, exits with 1 on failure, creates a global alias, and sets the global editor.
